author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
# Charter — Repo-wide public-API + data-contract docstring coverage

## Goal
Raise documentation coverage to 100% across the public API and data-contract
surface of `src/` and `packages/`, and make that level mandatory by extending
the existing static docstring gate so it can never regress.

## Scope (chosen tier: "Public API + data contracts")
Mandatory TSDoc on:
- every module (`@module` header) in `src/` and `packages/` (non-`.d.ts`, non-spec);
- every exported top-level declaration, INCLUDING exported `const`/`let`/`var`
  (the previous gate only required exported functions/classes/interfaces/types/enums);
- every member of every exported `interface`;
- every property member of every exported object-literal `type` alias;
- every PUBLIC member (method/accessor/property + parameterized constructor) of
  every exported `class`.

Out of scope (documented only where non-obvious, NOT gate-enforced): non-exported
internal helpers/consts, private/protected/`#` class members, members of
non-exported interfaces/types/classes, enum members.

## Baseline (measured 2026-07-04, TypeScript AST probe)
- Enforced gate before this epic: 100% for src module docs + src top-level exports.
- Chosen-tier coverage: 2783/6740 documented (41.3%); **3957 missing** across 188 files.
- Dominant buckets: interface members 3566, exported consts 316, package module
  docs 19, package exports ~53, exported type/interface/class headers ~15.

## Quality bar (best practice / TSDoc + TypeDoc)
- Describe WHAT the symbol represents and WHY it exists / how it is used across
  the CLI/SDK/MCP/storage surfaces — never restate the identifier name.
- State units, invariants, defaults, nullability, and side effects where relevant.
- Cross-reference related symbols with `{@link}`; reference settings keys and
  flags in backticks.
- No boilerplate; the gate rejects template stubs and name-only docstrings.

## Enforcement
`scripts/release/static-quality-gate.mjs` extended to measure and require 100% of
the above across `src/` + `packages/`, plus a conservative trivial-stub check.
Regression coverage added to `tests/unit/scripts/release/static-quality-gate.spec.ts`
(the gate script is itself under the 100% coverage requirement).

## Acceptance
- Static quality gate reports the new coverage checks at 100% with zero missing
  and zero boilerplate/trivial-stub violations.
- `pnpm build`, typecheck, gate spec, and `pnpm contracts:*` remain green.
Repo-wide 100% docstring coverage: public API + data contracts (gate-enforced)

Raise TSDoc coverage to 100% across the exported/public-API and data-contract surface of src/ and packages/, and extend the static docstring gate so 100% is mandatory and cannot regress. Chosen tier: module docs + every exported declaration (incl consts) + every exported interface/type member + public class members, across src/ AND packages/. Baseline measured 2026-07-04: 2783/6740 (41.3%), 3957 missing across 188 files. Duplicate check: pm search 'docstring'/'code documentation' found only the closed pm-p4mw (src-only, top-level-exports-only gate) and closed coverage items; no open item covers packages, exported consts, or interface/type members — this epic extends that lineage.

Static quality gate enforces 100% for: module docs (src+packages), exported declarations incl consts (src+packages), exported interface/type members, public class members; boilerplate+trivial-stub checks pass; gate spec at 100% coverage; pnpm build + typecheck + contracts green.

message: Create docstring-coverage epic; extends closed pm-p4mw to packages + consts + interface/type members

This incident is the evidence base for pm-w8q4 (stale in-progress detection).

patch.value: Repo-wide 100% public API and data-contract docstring program completed and gate-enforced across src and shipped packages.
Repo-wide documentation contract completed and enforced by mandatory static quality gates.
Every in-scope public API and data-contract symbol is documented and regression-gated.
Static gate reports 100% module/export/member docstring coverage with zero trivial or boilerplate violations; release gates and exact 100% code coverage pass.
